data.body: ROOT-CAUSE (confirm/deny of the cached-Pass-1 hypothesis): DENIED as causal. Tracing the path — plan_review_verify_inputs (workflow_ops.py:278) builds instructions as [verify_instructions(c) for c in (chunks or [[]])], so the PRODUCER always emits >=1 element even when every finding is omitted; and prompt caching (runs.py resolve_prompt_cached) does not touch the findings/instructions payload, so a fully-cache-served Pass-1 cannot by itself empty the instructions list. The ~21ms/llm_calls=26 the reporter saw is a LATENCY correlation of a cache-hit finder batch, not the cause. The actual defect is the CONSUMER contract: RunnerAgentStep.run treated an empty chunks list as 'run the model zero times' and _merge_chunked_outputs([]) returned {} as a clean success, so verify_requests=0 and every finding degraded to no-verification with empty stderr — indistinguishable from a healthy review. FIX (landed via change 304, bed48d6d8): the consumer now logs a diagnostic and dispatches one aggregate call on an empty instructions list (never silent), with a regression test. The precise transient by which instructions reached [] in the single reported f93a run was not reproducible offline, but the guard makes a silent zero-dispatch impossible regardless of origin.
